response.justification: Wei Chen is an experienced full-stack and systems developer with a broad background, but is fundamentally misaligned with this role on every critical dimension. The hard requirement for native-level Japanese proficiency is entirely absent from the resume. C programming is peripheral to the candidate's work history, with the bulk of experience in TypeScript, Node.js, and D-lang. There is no evidence of embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, Linux kernel modification, or any of the required security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal). The candidate's profile is that of a web/cloud engineer, not an embedded security firmware developer.
strengths: Long career (15+ years) demonstrating adaptability across multiple languages and domains, including some C/C++ at National Instruments
Experience with Linux and low-latency systems at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic shows comfort with systems-level thinking
Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, clean code) that would transfer to any engineering role
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a non-negotiable hard requirement and alone disqualifies the candidate
Zero experience with embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), or chip-vendor SDKs
C is not a primary language; career is almost entirely web/cloud (TypeScript, Node.js, D-lang), with no Linux kernel modification experience
